gaps.title: Draft Composer Race Condition
gaps.breaks: Multiple agents editing same draft simultaneously
gaps.scenario: Two agents viewing ticket #1234, both click "Edit" at same time, Socket.io broadcasts both versions, causing data corruption
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates two Socket.io connections to the same ticket ID, both attempting to edit the draft simultaneously. Verify that the system properly handles concurrent edits with proper locking or conflict resolution.

gaps.title: Training Data Cross-Contamination
gaps.scenario: Tenant A's variant "support_v1" gets mixed with Tenant B's variant "sales_v1" during vector index updates, causing incorrect draft suggestions
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates two tenants with different variants, trains models on different datasets, then verifies that draft suggestions only use the correct tenant's variant data by checking the vector index metadata and model configs.

gaps.title: Edge Case Handler Order Dependency
gaps.breaks: Incorrect ordering causes wrong handler to trigger
gaps.scenario: A query contains both "legal terminology" and "system commands", but the "system commands" handler runs first due to incorrect priority, blocking legitimate legal terminology processing
gaps.prompt: Write a test that sends queries containing multiple edge case triggers in different orders, verifying that the handler priority system correctly routes each case to the appropriate handler regardless of input order.

gaps.title: Cache Invalidation Race Condition
gaps.breaks: Stale data served after KB update
gaps.scenario: KB update triggers cache invalidation, but a concurrent request gets the old cached data before invalidation completes, leading to outdated draft suggestions
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simultaneously triggers a KB update and makes multiple concurrent requests for draft suggestions, verifying that all requests receive the updated data and no stale data is served after the update.

gaps.title: Technique Tier Access Bypass
gaps.breaks: Higher tier techniques accessed by lower tier tenants
gaps.scenario: A Starter tier tenant somehow accesses High tier techniques by manipulating variant tags in requests, bypassing the tier check
gaps.prompt: Write a test that attempts to access techniques across different tiers by manipulating request parameters, verifying that the tier access control is properly enforced at every point of technique selection.

gaps.title: Draft Session State Loss
gaps.breaks: In-memory draft session data lost on restart
gaps.scenario: Redis restarts while agents are actively editing drafts, causing all in-progress draft sessions to be lost without recovery
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a Redis restart while multiple draft sessions are active, verifying that the system properly persists draft session state and can recover without data loss.

gaps.title: Webhook Double-Fire Handling
gaps.scenario: Paddle sends duplicate "training_data_updated" webhook events, causing redundant retraining and potential index corruption
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates duplicate webhook events for training data updates, verifying that the system properly handles idempotency and doesn't process the same event twice.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 7

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Draft Composer Race Condition
What breaks: Multiple agents editing same draft simultaneously
Real scenario: Two agents viewing ticket #1234, both click "Edit" at same time, Socket.io broadcasts both versions, causing data corruption
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates two Socket.io connections to the same ticket ID, both attempting to edit the draft simultaneously. Verify that the system properly handles concurrent edits with proper locking or conflict resolution.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Training Data Cross-Contamination
What breaks: Variant datasets leak between tenants
Real scenario: Tenant A's variant "support_v1" gets mixed with Tenant B's variant "sales_v1" during vector index updates, causing incorrect draft suggestions
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two tenants with different variants, trains models on different datasets, then verifies that draft suggestions only use the correct tenant's variant data by checking the vector index metadata and model configs.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Edge Case Handler Order Dependency
What breaks: Incorrect ordering causes wrong handler to trigger
Real scenario: A query contains both "legal terminology" and "system commands", but the "system commands" handler runs first due to incorrect priority, blocking legitimate legal terminology processing
AI agent prompt: Write a test that sends queries containing multiple edge case triggers in different orders, verifying that the handler priority system correctly routes each case to the appropriate handler regardless of input order.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Cache Invalidation Race Condition
What breaks: Stale data served after KB update
Real scenario: KB update triggers cache invalidation, but a concurrent request gets the old cached data before invalidation completes, leading to outdated draft suggestions
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simultaneously triggers a KB update and makes multiple concurrent requests for draft suggestions, verifying that all requests receive the updated data and no stale data is served after the update.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: Technique Tier Access Bypass
What breaks: Higher tier techniques accessed by lower tier tenants
Real scenario: A Starter tier tenant somehow accesses High tier techniques by manipulating variant tags in requests, bypassing the tier check
AI agent prompt: Write a test that attempts to access techniques across different tiers by manipulating request parameters, verifying that the tier access control is properly enforced at every point of technique selection.

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Draft Session State Loss
What breaks: In-memory draft session data lost on restart
Real scenario: Redis restarts while agents are actively editing drafts, causing all in-progress draft sessions to be lost without recovery
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a Redis restart while multiple draft sessions are active, verifying that the system properly persists draft session state and can recover without data loss.

GAP 7
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Webhook Double-Fire Handling
What breaks: Duplicate training data update events
Real scenario: Paddle sends duplicate "training_data_updated" webhook events, causing redundant retraining and potential index corruption
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates duplicate webhook events for training data updates, verifying that the system properly handles idempotency and doesn't process the same event twice.
